creator: tomek.cedro@gmail.com
text:
There are some strange dependencies when building boost-1.41. My
system use python2.6, while boos-python-libs wants to use
py25-elementtree-1.2.6_1 and it conflicts with
py26-elementtree-1.2.6_1. Shouldnt boost-python-libs depend on py26?

# make install
===>  Installing for boost-all-1.41.0
===>   boost-all-1.41.0 depends on file:
/usr/local/lib/libboost_thread.so - found
===>   boost-all-1.41.0 depends on file:
/usr/local/lib/libboost_python.so - not found
===>    Verifying reinstall for /usr/local/lib/libboost_python.so in
/usr/ports/devel/boost-python-libs
===>   boost-python-libs-1.41.0 depends on file:
/usr/local/bin/python2.5 - found
===>   boost-python-libs-1.41.0 depends on executable: bjam - found
===>   boost-python-libs-1.41.0 depends on file:
/usr/local/lib/python2.5/site-packages/elementtree/ElementTree.py -
not found
===>    Verifying reinstall for
/usr/local/lib/python2.5/site-packages/elementtree/ElementTree.py in
/usr/ports/devel/py-elementtree
===>  Installing for py25-elementtree-1.2.6_1
===>   py25-elementtree-1.2.6_1 depends on file:
/usr/local/bin/python2.5 - found
===>   Generating temporary packing list
===>  Checking if devel/py-elementtree already installed
===>   An older version of devel/py-elementtree is already installed
(py26-elementtree-1.2.6_1)
      You may wish to ``make deinstall'' and install this port again
      by ``make reinstall'' to upgrade it properly.
      If you really wish to overwrite the old port of devel/py-elementtree
      without deleting it first, set the variable "FORCE_PKG_REGISTER"
      in your environment or the "make install" command line.
